channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump suspends U.S. naval escort operation in Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump has suspended the U.S. military operation known as Project Freedom, which aimed to protect commercial vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The decision came after warni...

item.title: Volcanic Ash from Mayon Disrupts 132 Schools in Albay
item.description: Following the recent eruption of Mayon Volcano in Albay, volcanic ash has affected 132 schools, while 52,590 families have been displaced and are currently staying in evacuation centers. Phivolcs repo...

item.title: Democrat Chedrick Greene Wins Michigan Special Election, Maintaining State Senate Majority
item.description: Democrat Chedrick Greene has won the special election for Michigan’s 35th Senate District, securing the Democratic Party’s control of the state Senate for the remainder of Governor Gretchen Whitmer’s ...

item.title: Suspect Denies Knowledge of Coup, Says He Was Misled in Court Video
item.description: For Abuja, one Zekeri Umoru wey dey work for maintenance department of Presidential Villa through construction company don appear for Federal High Court over alleged coup plot. For video wey court pla...

item.title: BJP warns its workers against post-election violence in West Bengal
item.description: Following the Bharatiya Janata Party's historic win in the West Bengal assembly elections, state BJP president Samik Bhattacharya on Tuesday strongly urged authorities to take prompt action against an...

item.title: Fadiel Adams arrested over fraud and investigation interference; NCC raises legal concerns
item.description: Fadiel Adams, the leader of the National Coloured Congress (NCC) and Member of Parliament, was arrested on Tuesday over serious allegations of fraud and defeating the ends of justice. The arrest, whic...

item.title: Most Britons fear rising food prices due to Iran conflict and energy costs
item.description: A recent Opinium poll reveals that 80% of Britons are worried that the ongoing conflict in Iran will drive up food prices, with 73% expecting the conflict to affect other products as well. The blockad...

item.title: Twice-Convicted Con Artist David Bloom Pleads No Contest to Multiple Fraud Charges in Los Angeles
item.description: David Bloom, a 62-year-old twice-convicted con artist known as the 'Wall Street Whiz Kid,' pleaded no contest in Los Angeles to 18 counts of grand theft by deception and securities fraud. Authorities ...

item.title: Four Palestine Action activists convicted for 2024 UK Elbit site break-in
item.description: In London, a jury has convicted four activists associated with Palestine Action for causing criminal damage at the UK offices of Israeli defence firm Elbit Systems in 2024. The case is the first convi...

item.title: What libraries, schools and readers should do with Craig Silvey's books after his guilty plea
item.description: Australian author Craig Silvey, known for Jasper Jones, Honeybee and Runt, has pleaded guilty to possessing and distributing child exploitation material, with two further charges dropped, including an...

item.title: Kwankwaso’s political moves and their impact on northern unity ahead of 2027
item.description: This opinion piece take a hard look at Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso political journey and argue say him actions don contribute to division for Northern Nigeria, especially as 2027 election dey approach. The w...

item.title: Greg Brockman Testifies on 2017 Confrontation with Elon Musk at OpenAI Trial
item.description: During the ongoing high-stakes trial regarding OpenAI’s transition from a nonprofit organization to a for-profit entity, OpenAI President Greg Brockman provided significant testimony on Tuesday. Brock...

item.title: Gray Davis Critiques Wealth Tax and Reflects on Gov. Newsom's Tenure
item.description: Former California Governor Gray Davis appeared on CNN to discuss his views on wealth taxation and evaluate the performance of current Governor Gavin Newsom. Davis expressed strong skepticism about the...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Economic Abuse Conviction Requires Proof of Paternity
item.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that in cases of economic abuse under the Anti-Violence Against Women and Their Children (VAWC) Law, a conviction cannot be secured without proof of paternity. This decisio...

item.title: Sindh High Court Rules Abidi Killing as Targeted Murder, Not Terrorism
item.description: KARACHI: The Sindh High Court on Tuesday clarified that the killing of former MQM-P MNA Ali Raza Abidi in December 2018 cannot be classified as terrorism. A two-judge bench, led by Justice Omar Sial, ...

item.title: Government stops funding Mama Joy’s World Cup trips due to high costs
item.description: Sports Minister Gayton McKenzie has announced that the South African government will no longer cover expenses for super fan Joyce “Mama Joy” Chauke to attend sporting events, citing excessive costs. D...

item.title: Green Party leader Zack Polanski accused of misrepresenting charitable and professional roles
item.description: Zack Polanski, the leader of the Green Party, has come under scrutiny for falsely claiming to be a spokesperson for the British Red Cross. The charity confirmed that Polanski has never held such a pos...

item.title: Kenyan Mum and Her Son Receive Official Invite to Visit Dubai by Crown Prince Hamdan
item.description: A Kenyan mother, Maria Nyambane, and her son are set to travel to Dubai after receiving an official invitation from Crown Prince Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum. The invitation followed shortly after t...

item.title: Rays Rally in Eighth to Defeat Blue Jays 4-3
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays suffered a narrow 4-3 defeat against the Tampa Bay Rays on Tuesday night in St. Petersburg, Florida, marking their second consecutive loss. The Rays extended their winning streak...

item.title: Nepra Cancels Rs42 Billion Penalties on NTDC After Legal Review
item.description: In a significant development, the National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Nepra) has reversed penalties worth Rs42 billion that were previously imposed on the National Transmission and Dispatch ...
